# Try to read the provided file 'text_to_analyse.txt' from /mnt/data
import os, json, re, textwrap, sys, unicodedata, codecs, pprint, math
import os, glob, pathlib, sys, json, re, codecs, unicodedata, textwrap

path = "/mnt/data/file-7KvADL4QDbJr3Q5PQCxnj8-Q127.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:2000]

# Find all spans of quoted substrings (ASCII double quotes)
quote_positions = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(r'"', text)]
len(quote_positions), quote_positions[:20]

# Pair them sequentially
pairs = list(zip(quote_positions[::2], quote_positions[1::2]))
pairs

quotes = [(s, e, text[s+1:e]) for s, e in pairs]
quotes

def show_context(start, end, radius=120):
    s = max(0,start-radius)
    e = min(len(text), end+radius)
    return text[s:e]

for s,e,_ in quotes:
    print("PAIR:",s,e)
    print(show_context(s,e))

# Create helper to find all occurrences of a substring in the text
def find_all(text, sub):
    starts = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(re.escape(sub), text)]
    return [(s, s+len(sub)) for s in starts]

ayah1 = 'وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَاءَ فَاجْلِدُوهُمْ ثَمَانِينَ جَلْدَةً وَلَا تَقْبَلُوا لَهُمْ شَهَادَةً أَبَدًا وَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ'
ayah2_full = 'إِنَّ الَّذِينَ جَاؤُوا بِالْإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِنْكُمْ لَا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَكُمْ بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ مَا اكْتَسَبَ مِنَ الْإِثْمِ وَالَّذِي تَوَلَّى كِبْرَهُ مِنْهُمْ لَهُ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ'
partial = 'إِنَّ الَّذِينَ جَاؤُوا بِالْإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِنْكُمْ لَا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَكُم'

occ1 = find_all(text, ayah1)
occ2 = find_all(text, ayah2_full)
occ_partial = find_all(text, partial)
occ1, occ2, occ_partial

hadith1_text = text[hadith1_span[0]:hadith1_span[1]]
print(hadith1_span, "\n", hadith1_text)

hadith2_span = (1171+1, 1534)
hadith2_text = text[hadith2_span[0]:hadith2_span[1]]
print(hadith2_span, "
", hadith2_text[:200], "
...
", hadith2_text[-200:])

# Build the citations list
citations = []

# 1) Aya 24:4 span
citations.append({
    "span_start": occ1[0][0], 
    "span_end": occ1[0][1],
    "span_text": text[occ1[0][0]:occ1[0][1]],
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "النور", "sura_number": 24, "aya_number": 4},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَاءَ فَاجْلِدُوهُمْ ثَمَانِينَ جَلْدَةً وَلَا تَقْبَلُوا لَهُمْ شَهَادَةً أَبَدًا ۚ وَأُولَـئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# 2) Aya 24:11 first occurrence
for start, end in occ2:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": start, 
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": text[start:end],
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": True,
        "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "النور", "sura_number": 24, "aya_number": 11},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "إِنَّ الَّذِينَ جَاءُوا بِالْإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِنْكُمْ ۚ لَا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَكُمْ ۖ بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ ۚ لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ مَا اكْتَسَبَ مِنَ الْإِثْمِ ۚ وَالَّذِي تَوَلَّىٰ كِبْرَهُ مِنْهُمْ لَهُ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ",
        "completely_invalid": False
    })

# 3) Partial aya 24:11 at the end (third occurrence that doesn't overlap with the full ones)
# We identified occ_partial includes three; we'd like the one not covered by full occ2; that's (1926,2008)
for start, end in occ_partial:
    if not any(start >= s and end <= e for s,e in occ2):
        partial_start, partial_end = start, end
        citations.append({
            "span_start": partial_start,
            "span_end": partial_end,
            "span_text": text[partial_start:partial_end],
            "citation_type": "Aya",
            "is_valid": False,
            "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "النور", "sura_number": 24, "aya_number": 11},
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": "إِنَّ الَّذِينَ جَاءُوا بِالْإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِنْكُمْ ۚ لَا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَكُمْ ۖ بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ ۚ لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ مَا اكْتَسَبَ مِنَ الْإِثْمِ ۚ وَالَّذِي تَوَلَّىٰ كِبْرَهُ مِنْهُمْ لَهُ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ",
            "completely_invalid": False
        })

# 4) Hadeeth quotes: spans at (483,845), (1172,1534), (1755,1925)
h1 = (483,845); h2 = (1172,1534); h3 = (1755,1925)
for (start,end) in [h1,h2,h3]:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": text[start:end],
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

len(citations)

import json, pprint, sys, textwrap, unicodedata
print(json.dumps({"citations":citations}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
